title: Train a true query-group ranker on within-lap decisions
summary: Official CatBoost and XGBoost ranking docs support group-aware pairwise/listwise losses.
rationale: Official CatBoost and XGBoost ranking docs support group-aware pairwise/listwise losses. Pit-stop choice is partly relative within each race-lap field state, so grouping by `Year/Race/LapNumber` matches the real decision surface better than global binary loss. This is a more concrete extension of earlier ranking ideas because it uses native query objectives rather than only post-hoc rank blending.
implementation_hint: Build `qid = Year + Race + LapNumber`, sort by `qid`, and train one model with `rank:pairwise` or `rank:ndcg` in XGBoost and/or `QueryCrossEntropy` or `QuerySoftMax` in CatBoost. Keep the current binary model, then blend OOF predictions from the ranker and classifier. Upweight groups containing at least one actual pit to avoid spending too much signal on all-zero laps.
expected_effect: Likely small-to-moderate gain via a more diverse expert that improves ordering among same-lap competitors, which should help ROC AUC after blending.
risk: Many groups may have no positives, so the ranker can be noisy unless grouping, weighting, and sorting are handled carefully.
